column3: A physical storage unit or container for samples (e.g., slide, capsule, box, drawer, cabinet).
Document Note: Sample Storage Model Overview

column1: Document Note: Sample Storage Model Overview
column3: This model describes a physical storage unit for heritage samples, including identifiers, human-readable labels, descriptive notes, storage type and status, references to relevant storage methods/protocols, its current keeper, and its location within a wider place hierarchy.
The institution or person currently responsible for the care or custody of this storage unit.
The place where the storage unit is located (e.g., room, store, facility).
Documents describing storage practice, handling, or even catalogues or logs.
A defined method or protocol applicable to storage or handling of samples.
Controlled type/category (e.g., slide, tray, box, drawer, cabinet).
Operational status (e.g., in service, decommissioned, quarantined, damaged).
The current condition state of the storage unit (e.g., good, fair, poor).
Link a procedure or SOP; if also published as a document, connect that record under Storage Documents.

column1: // Optional: storage hierarchy (drawer in cabinet in room)
